title: HK-24 series assassin droid
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{DroidSeries |image=[[File:HK-24-KotOR13cover.png]] |name=HK-24 series assassin droid |creator=[[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]]''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' |manufacturer=Czerka Corporation |designer= |homeworld= |line=[[HK-series assassin droid]] |model= |class=[[Assassin droid/Legends|Assassin droid]] |degree=Fourth-degree |cost=Not available for sale {{C|Estimated value: 24,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]}} |length= |width= |height= |mass= |gender= |sensor=[[Color/Legends|Orange]] |plating=Red [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]] armor plating |armament=*[[Blaster rifle/Legends|Blaster rifle]] *[[Self-destruct mechanism/Legends|Self-destruct mechanism]]{{TORcite|exp=lots|type=general|The Memory Heist|Ord Mantell}} |equipment=*[[Heuristic processor/Legends|Heuristic processor]] *[[Translator unit]] *[[Vocabulator/Legends|Vocabulator]] |affiliation=[[The Adasca BioMechanical Corporation of Arkania]][[Knights of the Old Republic 19|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 19]] |firstmade= |retired= }} {{Quote|Objective must not be damaged—and HK-24 intends to damage ''you''!|HK-24 threatening Jarael|[[Knights of the Old Republic 14|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 14]]}} The '''HK-24 series assassin droid''' was a class of [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] produced by the [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]] in the era of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. Known for their distinctive [[Color/Legends|red]] [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]] plating and fierce autonomy, the HK-24 was designed to impersonate [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droids]] to avoid suspicion and easier facilitate the elimination of its target. Although the HK-24 represented a significant advance in [[Droid/Legends|droid]] technology at the [[Time/Legends|time]], and was mass-produced by Czerka in the run-up to the Mandalorian Wars, a lack of mass commercial appeal for an assassin droid ensured the HK-24 was a massive financial failure. However, Czerka recouped some of its financial losses when the entire production run of remaining, unsold HK-24s was bought by [[The Adasca BioMechanical Corporation of Arkania|Adascorp]] [[Chief Executive Officer/Legends|CEO]] [[Arkoh Adasca]] in [[3963 BBY]], so he could employ the droids in his search for runaway [[Arkanian Offshoot/Legends|Arkanian Offshoot]] scientist [[Gorman Vandrayk]]. Adasca used many of these droids as security aboard his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the ''[[Arkanian Legacy]]'', but all of his HK-24s were destroyed when Vandrayk set a host of massive [[Exogorth/Legends|exogorth]] beasts upon the [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian's]] [[Starship|ship]]. Those that had survived, due to being purchased by planetary governments prior to Adasca's mass purchase, were eventually destroyed or locked away when the Galactic Republic declared the model illegal. ==Description== {{Quote|An experiment gone wrong… just autonomous enough to resent the blazes out of the existence of other units just like them.|Anonymous Czerka employee|{{KOTORbackups|20}}}} [[File:HK-24_assassin_droid.jpg|thumb|left|200px|An HK-24 unit assassinates a target.]] A class of [[Assassin droid/Legends|assassin droid]] designed by the [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]], the HK-24 represented one of the deadliest advances in [[Droid/Legends|droid]] technology of the era of the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. Despite their [[Color/Legends|red]] [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]] armor plating and sinister-looking orange visual [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]], HK-24s appeared to be common [[Service droid/Legends|service droids]] to the untrained [[Eye/Legends|eye]], but that was intentional on Czerka's part. HK-24s were in fact designed to impersonate [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droids]] to lure their targets into a false sense of security and make the eventual [[Death/Legends|kill]] that much easier. Despite their unremarkable appearance, HK-24s were deadly with a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]] and highly cunning, possessing a [[Heuristic processor/Legends|heuristic processor]] to boost an HK-24's learning and reasoning and outfitted with a [[translator unit]] that allowed it to understand millions of forms of communication. In hand-to-hand combat, HK-24s could utilize a [[utility vibroblade]] that helped the assassin droid specialize in cutting off [[Limb/Legends|limbs]]. HK-24s could be effectively used not only as assassins, but as [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], although their use earned criticism from organic hunters in the era of the Mandalorian Wars. HK-24s also saw use as security— their durasteel frame was quite sturdy, able to withstand crushing hits from much larger opponents, such as [[LB-series bulk-loading droid|bulk-loading droids]], and lose little if any functionality. However, HK-24s could not always be counted on to work together. The droids were fiercely autonomous and resented others of the same class, as each HK-24 believed itself above its "redundant" and "artifact" peers. Grouping several HK-24s together often simply resulted in the droids bickering and insulting one another. ==History== {{Quote|These droids! One of them boarded our ship on Ralltiir. You sent it?''"
"''When we learned that Gorman Vandrayk—that ''Camper'' yet lived, I bought the ''entire production run'' of HK-24s and sent them to nearby systems.|Jarael and Arkoh Adasca|}} The first major success in Czerka Corporation's assassin droid production, the HK-24 was mass-produced in the hopes that demand from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] governments would be high in the run-up to the Mandalorian Wars between the Republic and the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders]]. Czerka advertized the droids to planetary governments as a form of defense against the impending threat of the Neo-Crusaders, and even made some successful sales.{{TORcite|exp=lots|type=codex|HK-24}} Czerka overestimated their market, however—most people in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] simply did not have a need for an assassin droid in their day-to-day lives—and the HK-24 wound up as a financial failure, with their production facilities eventually being shut down and abandoned. Although the HK-24 was not a success for Czerka, the droids wound up being put to great use by one of the Republic's captains of industry: [[Arkoh Adasca]], the [[Chief Executive Officer/Legends|CEO]] of [[The Adasca BioMechanical Corporation of Arkania|Adascorp]], the galaxy's largest bioengineering firm. With the war looming, Adasca had a plan to turn his corporation into a galactic player, by selling the destructive services of the massive, monstrous space slug [[Species/Legends|species]] known as the [[Exogorth/Legends|exogorths]] to one of the competing factions in the war, effectively choosing the winner. However, this plot had no chance of success without the knowledge of one particular [[Arkanian Offshoot/Legends|Arkanian Offshoot]] scientist named [[Gorman Vandrayk]], who had fled Adascorp over thirty [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before when he realized the company was planning on weaponizing the beasts.[[Knights of the Old Republic 20|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 20]] [[File:Jarael_protects_Camper.jpg|thumb|right|200px|An HK-24 attempts to capture Gorman Vandrayk aboard ''The Last Resort''.]] After a long search, Adasca's [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] aide [[Eejee Vamm]] discovered in [[3963 BBY]] that Vandrayk was alive and had emerged from hiding after sighting him in security footage from the [[Bank/Legends|banking]] world of [[Telerath/Legends|Telerath]].[[Knights of the Old Republic 13|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 13]] In response, Adasca purchased the entire production run of remaining HK-24s and sent them to nearby [[Star system/Legends|systems]] to track him down. Soon afterward, one HK-24 unit found the elusive scientist on the backwoods world of [[Ralltiir/Legends|Ralltiir]], managing to gain entry to his [[junk hauler]], [[The Last Resort (starship)|''The Last Resort'']], concealed in a crate of [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] parts. The HK-24 emerged from its hiding place once the ship jumped to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], quickly identifying a shocked Vandrayk, along with his close friend and protector, [[Jarael]]. HK-24 needed Vandrayk alive, but deemed the other Offshoot to be immaterial to his objective and decided to eliminate her. Jarael leapt in to protect Vandrayk, and as the HK-24 approached—intending to carve the Offshoot up with its [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]]—the assassin droid was attacked and sent flying across the cargo hold by Vandrayk's massive bulk-loading droid, [[T1-LB]]. The HK-24 recovered, drew its blaster rifle and incapacitated the droid with a series of [[Laser/Legends|laser]] blasts, only for Jarael to attack it from behind with a chain. Jarael managed to destroy one of the HK-24's sensors, but the droid had gained the upper hand when it was destroyed in a surprise attack from behind by the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] warrior [[Demagol|Rohlan Dyre]], an acquaintance of Vandrayk's and Jarael's who had secretly stowed away on their ship. Not long afterward, however, Vandrayk was forced to return to [[Arkania/Legends|Arkania]] due to an illness, where Adasca was able to manipulate Jarael into helping lead them to the Offshoot scientist. Vandrayk was quickly brought back to health once aboard Adasca's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the ''[[Arkanian Legacy]]'',[[Knights of the Old Republic 17|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 17]] where he was put back to work on the exogorths—by order of Adasca, he was accompanied everywhere by a squad of HK-24 units to ensure compliance.[[Knights of the Old Republic 18|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 18]] Once the ''Arkanian Legacy'' reached [[Omonoth]], where a large number of dormant exogorths were located, the [[Time/Legends|time]] for the auction had finally come—invited parties from the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]], Mandalorians and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order's]] interventionist [[Revanchist]] faction flocked to the Adascorp flagship, and Adasca began using his HK-24 units openly as security. Adasca also directed several HK-24s to secure the ship's [[Prison/Legends|brig]] after acquiring a pair of high-profile prisoners: [[Zayne Carrick]], the alleged perpetrator of the [[Padawan Massacre]] of [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], who arrived in the custody of the Republic delegation, and [[Lucien Draay]], a [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who had been [[Poison/Legends|poisoned]] and incapacitated by Adasca after he unexpectedly crashed the auction. Guarded by five HK-24 units, Carrick and Draay managed to overpower their captors and escape, and formulated a plan with Republic [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Carth Onasi]] to disrupt the auction and foil Adasca's power grab. With Carrick posing as a Mandalorian, the prisoners and Onasi were able to create a distraction allowing Draay to [[First Battle of Omonoth|attack]] and cut through Draay's personal guard of HK-24s. Meanwhile, Vandrayk reprogrammed the HK-24 watching him to destroy the exogorth command station as he escaped and directed the massive beasts to tear the ''Arkanian Legacy'' apart. Adasca still had several dozen HK-24 units defending him to the end, but they were all destroyed along with their master as the exogorths obliterated the ship and ended his dreams of domination.[[Knights of the Old Republic 21|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 21]] Despite this development, HK-24 units still saw action during the Mandalorian Wars.{{TORcite|exp=lots|type=general|Eyes on the Prize|Ord Mantell}} Those that hadn't been bought by Adasca found themselves fighting against the Mandalorians just as Czerka had promised. The droids proved extremely efficient, killing many Mandalorians and destroying hundreds of [[Basilisk war droid/Legends|Basilisk war droids]]. At some point not long after the Mandalorian Wars, the HK-24 model was considered illegal and found only rarely around the galaxy. HK-24 units were either destroyed or locked away. A certain unit was found harassing civilians on the planet [[Nouane/Legends|Nouane]], and was stored in a cell on the prison planet known as [[Belsavis/Legends|Belsavis]]. Rumours spread of its imprisonment there, but could not be proven. Several [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the end of the war, during the [[Jedi Civil War]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] based his design of the [[HK-47]] assassin droid off the HK-24, adding several of his own upgrades. The HK-47 in turn became the basis for the [[HK-50 series assassin droid|HK-50 assassin droid]], which was produced en masse by Revan's [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]] during the war. [[File:HK-24Belsavis.png|thumb|left|200px|One of the only HK-24 units left, stored in a cell on Belsavis]] By the time of the [[Third Galactic War]], only a few HK-24 units were known to still exist. Due to it having schematics for Basilisk war droids in its [[Memory bank/Legends|memory core]], the [[Alliance Commander]], with the help of a droid [[Collector/Legends|collector]] known as [[the Curator]], tracked down the HK-24 unit locked away on Belsavis in order to help [[Lane Vizla]] repair a Basilisk war droid that had been experimented on by [[Kur Ha'rangir]]. Following the retrieval of the core, the chassis was retrieved by retrieval experts working for the Curator. The Commander and Vizla installed the core into an [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]], but the [[Shebs|HK-24 astromech]] became extremely distressed and attempted to self-destruct, only for nothing to happen due to its new body possessing no such mechanism. Noticing the small Basilisk droid the two intended to repair, it attempted to follow its last directive and destroy the droid, so had to be stopped by Vizla. The Commander returned to the Curator, who was somewhat vexed by the choice of an astromech droid as a chassis, though he still agreed to help create a [[Restraining bolt/Legends|restraining bolt]] to make it comply.{{TORcite|exp=lots|type=general|Eyes on the Prize|Ord Mantell}} To modify and access the HK unit's heuristic processor, the Curator replaced the memory core into its original chassis. Unbeknownst to him, the droid was equipped with a reserve power supply, and activated immediately. The droid believed it had a mission to kill the [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] collector, and chased him into his panic room. The droid was ultimately subdued by the Commander and the memory core was removed and place back into the astromech, now fitted with a restraining bolt. Now under control, Lane and the Commander were able to interrogate it for the schematics for the war droids, though the astromech continually argued with itself over its actions.{{TORcite|exp=lots|type=general|A Few Modifications|Ord Mantell}} Lane decided to name the droid "Shebs," meaning "backside" in [[Mando'a/Legends|Mando'a]], to reflect how he was a pain.{{TORcite|exp=lots|type=message|Updates on our little friend|Lane Vizla}} ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Hk24formercover.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The HK-24 series made its debut in the ''Knights of the Old Republic'' comic series.]] The HK-24 assassin droid first appeared in [[Knights of the Old Republic 13|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 13]], an issue of the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[John Jackson Miller]] and released in [[2007]]. After the droid's second appearance in [[Knights of the Old Republic 14|the series' next issue]], Miller revealed that the HK-24's number was inspired by [[Wikipedia:NASCAR|NASCAR]] driver [[Wikipedia:Jeff Gordon|Jeff Gordon]] who, sponsored by [[Lucasfilm]], drove a #24 car bearing the image of [[Yoda]] to a victory in the 2005 [[Wikipedia:GEICO 500|Aaron's 499]]—Miller kept a miniature model of Gordon's Yoda car on his writing desk.{{Faraway|url=comics/swknights/swkotor14.html|text=''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic #14''}} The HK-24 droid went on to appear through the ensuing story arcs [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Nights of Anger|''Nights of Anger'']] and [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Daze of Hate|''Daze of Hate'']], and over the course of the droid's appearances in the series it was illustrated by [[Brian Ching]], [[Harvey Tolibao]] and [[Bong Dazo]]. ==Appearances== *[[Knights of the Old Republic 13|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 13]] {{1st}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 14|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 14]] *[[Knights of the Old Republic 17|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 17]] *[[Knights of the Old Republic 18|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 18]] *[[Knights of the Old Republic 19|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 19]] *{{KOTORbackups|20}} *[[Knights of the Old Republic 20|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 20]] *[[Knights of the Old Republic 21|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 21]] *{{KOTORbackups|23}} {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Legacy of the Sith]]'' ==Sources== *''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[Scavenger's Guide to Droids]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=HK-24 |pl=Droid zabójca serii HK-24 }} [[Category:Czerka Arms products]] [[Category:Droids with masculine programming]] [[Category:HK-series assassin droid models]]
